title: NOC 31112 – Audiologists & Speech-Language Pathologists | GTR Immigration
description: Audiologists and Speech-Language Pathologists are essential healthcare professionals supporting communication, hearing, and swallowing health across Canada. With aging populations, increased early-childhood screening, and expanded rehabilitation services, demand continues to grow nationwide. Classified under NOC 31112 (TEER 1), this occupation aligns with:

classification.teer: TEER 1 eligibility for Express EntryStable public-sector employmentOpportunities in pediatric and geriatric carePrivate-practice potentialStrong interdisciplinary healthcare collaborationAccess to Canada’s public healthcare system

duties: Conduct audiometric testing and diagnostic assessments
Fit and adjust hearing aids and amplification devices
Diagnose speech, voice, fluency, and swallowing disorders
Conduct research and develop diagnostic tools
employment_requirements: Master’s degree in Speech-Language Pathology (for SLPs)
Registration with provincial regulatory bodies in provinces such as:OntarioAlbertaBritish ColumbiaQuebecManitobaSaskatchewanNew BrunswickNewfoundland and Labrador
Membership in the Canadian Association of Speech-Language Pathologists and Audiologists is usually required
In some provinces, audiologists require a separate license to dispense hearing aids
Without provincial registration, professionals cannot independently practice.
job_outlook: This suggests a stable employment outlook, especially in:

Typical annual earnings: $57,800–$101,000 CAD, depending on province, specialization, and experience.
Rural communities and school-based therapy services show particularly strong demand.
